body:
Title: CRA Form NT479 2025 Northwest Territories credit.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5012-tc/5012-tc-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5012-tc/5012-tc-25e.pdf

NT479 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 12000
Value: 48000
Value: 66000
Value: 0.026
Value: 0.0125
Value: 0.01
Value: 312
Value: 762
Value: 942
Value: 350

CRA Form NT479 2025 calculates the Northwest Territories cost of living tax credit. Lines 1 to 5 calculate adjusted net income by subtracting lines 24400 and 25000 from line 23600, flooring the result at zero, then subtracting foreign income from line 25600 that is exempt under a tax treaty and flooring again. Lines 6 to 13 calculate the basic credit for self. Column 1 applies to adjusted net income of $12,000 or less at 2.6%. Column 2 applies above $12,000 and not more than $48,000 at 1.25% plus a $312 base. Column 3 applies above $48,000 at 1% plus a $762 base. If adjusted net income is $66,000 or more, line 13 enters $942, and line 13 has a maximum of $942.

Lines 14 to 20 calculate the cost of living tax credit supplement. Line 14 gives a $350 basic supplement for self, and line 15 gives $350 for a spouse or common-law partner. Line 19 adds the taxpayer basic credit for self and spouse basic credit for self, if applicable. Line 20 is zero if the taxpayer was under 18 on December 31, 2025, or if the spouse or common-law partner claimed the supplement for the family; otherwise line 20 is line 16 minus line 19 floored at zero. Line 21 adds line 13 and line 20 and enters the Northwest Territories credit on T1 line 47900, with a maximum of $942.
